prevalence.trend: stable; detection high due to routine CBC use

burden.mortalityRate: Low overall; higher in severe secondary causes and bleeding

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Platelets <150,000/µL with evaluation for pseudothrombocytopenia and etiology; bleeding risk stratification and peripheral smear review

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: CBC-based detection in prenatal/hospital settings is common; no population screening
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: hundreds of thousands with transient or chronic forms
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; depends on cause and access to hematology and treatments

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: CBC widely available; specialized testing (ITP workup, marrow) concentrated in referral centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Treat underlying cause; ITP: steroids/IVIG and second-line agents; transfusion for bleeding/severe thrombocytopenia; manage dengue-associated thrombocytopenia supportively
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Etiology diversity and need for specialist evaluation; seasonal dengue surges increase cases
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Routine CBC availability and established referral pathways for severe cases
